report_name: RubyGems Halts New Signups Amid 'Major Malicious Attack' Involving Hundreds of Packages

attack_patterns.name: Compromise Software Supply Chain
attack_patterns.description: Unidentified attackers uploaded hundreds of malicious packages (gems) to the RubyGems registry to compromise developers who installed them. This is a classic software supply chain attack targeting the open-source ecosystem.

malware.name: Infostealer
malware.description: A portion of the malicious Ruby gems uploaded to the registry contained infostealers designed to steal credentials, source code, or cryptocurrency keys from developers' systems.

identities.name: RubyGems
identities.description: The official package manager for the Ruby programming language, which was the target of a large-scale supply chain attack. Its infrastructure was targeted, and it was forced to suspend new account registrations.

identities.name: Ruby Developers
identities.description: The user base of the RubyGems registry, targeted by malicious packages designed to compromise their development environments upon installation.

indicators.name: Suspicious Gem Installation Command
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious Gem Installation Command
id: 9c1b2d3e-4f5a-4b6c-8d9e-0f1a2b3c4d5e
status: experimental
description: Detects the execution of 'gem install', which could indicate the installation of a malicious Ruby package. This rule is broad and should be used for hunting and auditing purposes.
references:
  - https://thehackernews.com/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-10
tags:
  - attack.t1195.002
level: low
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: linux
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '/gem'
    CommandLine|contains: 'install'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the execution of `gem install` commands, which should be reviewed for any gems with typos or unfamiliar names as part of a supply chain compromise investigation.

indicators.name: Gemfile.lock Modification For Audit
indicators.pattern:
title: Gemfile.lock Modification For Audit
id: a1b2c3d4-e5f6-4a7b-8c9d-0e1f2a3b4c5d
status: experimental
description: Detects modifications to Gemfile.lock. This file defines the exact versions of gems to be installed and changes should be audited to prevent supply chain attacks.
references:
  - https://thehackernews.com/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-10
tags:
  - attack.t1195.002
level: informational
logsource:
  category: file_event
detection:
  selection:
    TargetFilename|endswith: 'Gemfile.lock'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects modifications to `Gemfile.lock`, which provides a definitive list of all installed gems and their versions. Changes to this file should be audited.

indicators.name: Anomalous Network Connection from Gem Process
indicators.pattern:
title: Anomalous Network Connection from Gem Process
id: b2c3d4e5-f6a7-4b8c-9d0e-1f2a3b4c5d6e
status: experimental
description: Detects the Ruby 'gem' process making an outbound network connection to a destination other than the official rubygems.org registry. This could indicate a malicious gem exfiltrating data or downloading a second stage.
references:
  - https://thehackernews.com/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-10
tags:
  - attack.t1195.002
level: medium
logsource:
  category: network_connection
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith:
      - '/gem'
      - '/ruby'
    Initiated: true
  filter:
    DestinationHostname|endswith: 'rubygems.org'
  condition: selection and not filter
indicators.description: Detects outbound network connections from the `gem` process to domains other than the official RubyGems registry, which is highly suspicious and could indicate malicious activity from a compromised gem.

relationships.source_name: Anomalous Network Connection from Gem Process
